programmes.show_description: (2021) Guillermo del Toro's star-studded carny thriller with Bradley Cooper and Cate Blanchett. Can a chancer with a dark past use clairvoyancy to fleece rich New Yorkers out of a fortune?
Carol's rushed to Queen's after a lighter set fire to her oxygen tank and burnt her face. Trevor, who's blind, has fallen down the stairs at home. And Grace has injured her knee.
A steam engine enthusiast makes an ambitious journey across Devon in an 80-year-old steamroller. Near Falmouth, two champion free divers explore a spectacular underwater cave.

programmes.show_name: George Clarke's Old House New Home
programmes.show_description: George meets builder Anthony and physiotherapist Emily, who are determined to turn their ramshackle 17th-century cottage in Warwickshire into their family home.

programmes.show_name: Matt Baker: Our Farm in the Dales
programmes.show_description: Matt gets to work on a set of stone steps for the paddock and helps beekeeper George transport some of the beehives to the high Dales for heather season.

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: In Portugal, there's beer fear at Richard and Simon's new bar. Will they run out? A group of cyclists tackle the French Pyrenees' snowy mountains.
...the Sun: With Jon back to health, things finally start to look up. Work starts in the garden and Jasmine's carpenter brother Andrew helps out making bookshelves for Joy's bedroom. (Ep8)
Retiree Marie and her son Alex are searching for a permanent home for Marie and her cats in Poitou-Charentes in France. Can Scarlette Douglas find their purr-fect home for £110,000?
Tom and his son Alan look for a holiday home on beautiful Gran Canaria, that their whole family can enjoy. Can Ben Hillman find the perfect place for Tom's £120k budget?
Tim and Krys build a wine cave, but is Tim out of his depth? Alicia and Carlton race to prep for their first wedding. Tim and Sasha create a bold, colourful courtyard.

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or
programmes.show_description: List It: Brilliant Builds: Did Susan and John's Rushden renovation give them the social hub they wanted without losing any of their home's cherished charm?
North Devon. The first host, eco-campaigner Hector Christie, serves up a menu of raw food and home-grown produce. But his guests are more impressed by his stately home. (Ep1/5)
North Devon. Former cabaret queen and make-up artist Kirsten goes back to her roots with a Danish night. Her traditional menu is a hit, but what do the guests make of her singing? (Ep2/5)
North Devon. Sales representative Damion Fearnley aims to impress with a carefully crafted menu of restaurant-quality food. But there's tension between two of his guests. (Ep3/5)
North Devon. Theatre nurse Jenny Fox hopes to have the competition all sewn up by preparing her party with medical precision. But will her risqué entertainment prove too much? (Ep4/5)
North Devon. Retired housekeeper Viv Foster - who has years of experience serving the rich and famous - raises the stakes with a butler, silver service and super posh nosh. (Ep5/5)
Retirees Clover and Mark want to find a new home in one of the stunning villages of Axarquía in Andalucía. Danni Menzies lines up five superb villas for their budget of £300,000.
...for Stand Up To Cancer: Alex Brooker, Ambika Mod, Rose Ayling-Ellis, Vicky Pattison and Sam Thompson make mini rolls, syrup sponge puddings, and iconic outfits in choux.
Carol's rushed to Queen's after a lighter set fire to her oxygen tank and burnt her face. Trevor, who's blind, has fallen down the stairs at home. And Grace has injured her knee.
A stressed 76-year-old is rushed to St George's with a deep head cut. And a mother remembers learning to love the colour of her skin after suffering racial abuse growing up.
The Goggleboxers settle in for I'm a Celebrity... South Africa, Britain's Got Talent, The Dog House, The Apprentice, Secret Garden and the news on the Artemis II moon mission.
This time it's Race Across the World, Married at First Sight Australia, The Apprentice, Last One Laughing, Secrets of the Bees, Just One Thing and Celebrity Ex on the Beach.
Sparks fly as First Dates returns. A former Lioness meets a cat lover. A yoga teacher contemplates a mindfulness coach. And a doctor calms her nerves with a self-confessed geek.
...Experiment: Chelsea and George seek a threesome, but will it help Chelsea move past George's sexually adventurous past and infidelity? Jess and Ben host a naked dinner party.
Hannah & Mason's: Gordon Ramsay's rescue mission this time is Hannah & Mason's, a French bistro in the quaint little town of Cranbury, New Jersey.
Ordering a factory-built house was a no-brainer for manufacturing maestro Pete. But, in true self-building style, even a near-finished project brings challenges like a bat out of hell.

Llanelli. House-proud Charlotte plans a menu that reflects her personality and a rugby choir performance in her living room. (Ep1/5)
Llanelli. Electrician and bachelor Steve serves soup, lasagne and homemade wine, on a lively night featuring a shocking revelation. (Ep2/5)
Llanelli. Occupational nurse Christine serves up a menu of family favourites and a rock band performance. But then Ainsley tells Christine that she looks like one of her exes... (Ep3/5)
Llanelli. Retired French teacher Meic plans an authentic French menu. But what will he make of Charlotte turning up wearing an onion necklace? (Ep4/5)
Llanelli. Teaching assistant Ainsley hosts with an authentic Russian theme. Can her vodka-infused pavlova and some Russian dancing compensate for the cantankerous mood at the table? (Ep5/5)
Clacton-on-Sea. Trucker and foodie Tony Houston hopes to impress with a menu of scallops, sea bass and three desserts. But one guest gives off a prickly first impression. (Ep1/5)
Clacton-on-Sea. After annoying his rival diners with his cutlery obsession and questionable table manners on the first night, can sandwich shop owner Anthony turn things around? (Ep2/5)
Clacton-on-Sea. Driving instructor Claire Kelly confesses to a 'chaotic' style of cooking, but she's still confident that she can win with her enticing entertainment... (Ep3/5)
Clacton-on-Sea. Entrepreneur Richard's guests aren't impressed to discover that he's had help with foraging his ingredients. Can a choral performance calm the atmosphere? (Ep4/5)
Clacton-on-Sea. Teacher Kerrie Brinkley-Whittington plans a party atmosphere for the final evening in Essex. But the night ends in tears. (Ep5/5)

programmes.show_name: George Clarke's Old House New Home
programmes.show_description: Can Duncan and Josie transform their 18th-century barn into a liveable home before baby Ruby arrives? And can Jenny restore a Victorian gothic pile to its former glory?
We catch up with Carole and Alan, who needed a home with lots of land and holiday let potential in Somerset or Devon. And did Ros and Hannah find the perfect first home in London?
Donna and Martin from Dagenham want to buy a holiday home on the stunning Costa Dorada in Spain, on a budget of £65,000. Will any of Jasmine Harman's properties hit the mark?
Sue and Albert love spending quality time on holiday with their children and grandchildren. Can Lee Juggurnauth find the perfect bolthole for them in their beloved Benalmádena for £190k?
Tom and his son Alan look for a holiday home on beautiful Gran Canaria, that their whole family can enjoy. Can Ben Hillman find the perfect place for Tom's £120k budget?
Loretta and Carl want the perfect Spanish bolthole in Mojácar, where they can relax and unwind whenever they like. Laura Hamilton shows them what their £100k can buy.

programmes.show_name: Help! I Bought It at Auction...
programmes.show_description: ...with Sarah Beeny: In the South Downs National Park, Michelle and Derek take on a cosy cottage in need of some TLC. Archie and Lauren try to add value to their Somerset fixer-upper. (Ep3)
When Sarah Millican was a bairn, she wouldn't say boo to a goose. But now? She's goose booing all over the shop! Sarah's seventh stand-up special explores how one Sarah became the other.
Claymore: Spy thriller with Simu Liu. When top-secret intelligence agency The Orphanage becomes convinced that it has a mole, analyst Alexander Hale realises he's the prime suspect. (Ep1/8)
Glass House: Spy thriller. Alexander and The Orphanage are forced to work together to protect their discovery, setting a deadly trap with Alexander and Michelle as bait. (Ep2/8)
